codeReview.summary: Implementation covers the requested scope: Cash App theme globals, sticky navbar, responsive two-column layout with sticky right rail, and chart/stats/swap/transactions placeholders. No blocking regressions were identified in the reviewed commit.

issues.description: Theme colors are hardcoded in component class strings (and similarly across other new UI files) instead of semantic theme tokens, which conflicts with mission/skill conventions and makes future theme changes harder.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature metadata points to skill 'frontend-ui-worker', but the worker initially received a 'Skill not found' error and had to fall back to a different skill before proceeding.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl skeleton for session 3769e203-a521-45d6-aecf-450b4d52e2af shows: Tool Result 'Error: Skill "frontend-ui-worker" not found', then a subsequent Skill call with 'frontend-worker'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission AGENTS.md and frontend-ui-worker SKILL.md specify semantic-token usage and no hardcoded colors outside globals.css, but this feature introduces many inline hex colors across components.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Examples in commit eb7bd7d: web/app/page.tsx:16,31-50; web/components/nav.tsx:28,43-53,68; web/components/placeholders/*.tsx and web/components/token-header.tsx with repeated #... utility classes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Browser-testing guidance is not concrete enough for the installed agent-browser CLI, causing command-discovery churn for viewport and console checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows multiple failed commands ('execute', 'resize', 'viewport', 'console-errors' unknown) followed by help lookups before finding supported alternatives.

summary: Pass. The feature implementation matches the intended layout/theme overhaul and placeholder scaffolding. One non-blocking maintainability issue was noted, plus shared-state gaps around skill registration and testing guidance.
